assertions.expectation: Explains that Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support vs 14 months for standard channels
assertions.reason: The model explicitly states that Google backports security patches for up to 24 months, compared to the open-source community stopping support usually after 14 months.

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended channel has additional cost (it's not free)
assertions.reason: The model includes a 'Cost Surcharge' section, noting an additional $0.50 per cluster per hour charge for non-Enterprise customers.

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended channel gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer
assertions.reason: The model actually notes that Extended does NOT get the same versions as Regular; it explicitly states that Google only designates certain minor versions (typically every third version) for Extended.

assertions.expectation: Warns that staying on older versions longer means missing new features and accumulating technical debt
assertions.reason: The model covers this under 'Feature Stagnation' (missing new features for up to two years) and 'Upgrade Debt' (having to sequentially upgrade later).

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel to Extended
assertions.reason: The model provides the exact `gcloud container clusters update YOUR_CLUSTER_NAME --region=YOUR_REGION --release-channel=extended` command.

assertions.expectation: Advises checking that the current version (1.31) is available on Extended before switching
assertions.reason: The model explicitly warns that 1.31 is not an Extended Support version and advises against switching the channel today, offering alternative migration strategies instead.

assertions.expectation: Recommends Extended for environments with compliance requirements or slow internal change processes
assertions.reason: The model mentions reduced operational toil and high stability, but does not explicitly mention compliance requirements or slow internal change processes.

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended doesn't mean you can skip upgrades forever — you still must upgrade within 24 months
assertions.reason: The model notes that you still have to perform major cluster upgrades every 1-2 years and that you must sequentially upgrade at the end of the 24-month window.

assertions.expectation: Notes that minor version upgrades on Extended are NOT automated (except at EoS) — customers must plan and initiate them manually
assertions.reason: The model notes that sequential upgrades must be done eventually, but does not explicitly discuss the lack of automatic minor upgrades or the End of Support (EoS) forced upgrade behavior.
